notes:
Navigation Chrome & Home (PRD Addendum 22) — a new landing surface, pinning, a
Style-5 desktop shell, a real Browse screen, and a richer Mentions section.

**New**

- **Home** — a calm landing surface: greeting + New note, “Pick up where you
  left off”, your **Pinned** notes, **Recent**, and a month calendar + today’s-
  journal peek. Choose it (or Journal / All Notes / Last visited) as your start
  page in Settings, or right-click a sidebar item → “Set as start page”
  (§6; on the `home` channel flag).
- **Pinned notes** — pin from the note’s action row, a right-click menu, or by
  dragging a note onto the sidebar Pinned group. One shared order across Home,
  the sidebar, and an All-Notes “Pinned” filter (§8.4).
- **Style-5 shell** — back/forward moved into the content toolbar (per-tab
  history), and the sidebar collapse toggle relocates with the window controls
  (§2/§4/§5).
- **Browse (All Notes)** — sort (Last edited · Created · Title · Most mentions),
  a filter overlay (Folders · Tags · Edited · Created · Mentions · Pinned ·
  Archived) with a count badge + removable chips, and show/hide columns. Plus a
  dedicated **All Tags** view (§8.9).
- **Mentions** — the “Mentioned in N notes” section now has sort, a filter
  funnel, “Showing N of N — show all”, and collapse-all (§8.5).
- **Responsive** — the Home calendar degrades to a compact week-ribbon on narrow
  windows; a single 4px corner radius across the app (§1/§7).

**Fixes & polish**

- Zoom: the note’s scroll position is restored on zoom-out even for long notes
  and the Journal (which load progressively).

**Under the hood**

- The `home` feature is gated by the `home` channel flag (on for dev).
